name: Marbeled Double Chocolate Cheesecake Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line an 8-inch square baking pan with foil, extending the edges over the sides of the pan.
3. In a small mixing bowl, beat butter, 1/2 cup sugar, and salt until smooth.
4. Stir together flour and cocoa; gradually add to butter mixture, beating until soft dough is formed.
5. Press dough onto the bottom of the prepared pan.
6. Beat cream cheese and remaining 1/2 cup sugar until smooth.
7. Add eggs and vanilla; blend well.
8. In a separate bowl, mix 1 cup of the batter with chocolate topping, stirring until well blended.
9. Pour 1 cup of the flavored batter over the dough.
10. Stir melted chocolate into the remaining flavored batter; set aside.
11. Gently pour the vanilla batter, about 1/4 cup at a time, over the flavored batter.
12. Drop tablespoons of the reserved flavored batter over the top; gently swirl with a knife or spatula for a marbled effect.
13. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes, or until the cheesecake is firm and slightly puffed.
14. Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack; refrigerate.
15. To serve, lift from the pan using the foil edges; cut into squares. Garnish as desired.
Yield: about 20 squares
